conditioning: fusional: a single ending fuses tense/aspect/mood with person/number across the three conjugation classes (-ar/-er/-ir)

examples: subjunctive/conditional moods; person/number is fused into the same endings
fit_none.note: the pivot recovers the major tense/aspect/form distinctions; the full person×number×mood cross-product is fused and not separated

prose: spa marks tense/aspect/mood with FUSIONAL verb endings across three conjugation classes: present (-o/-a/-amos), preterite (-ó/-aron), imperfect (-aba/-ía), future (-rá/-ré), gerund (-ndo), past participle (-do). Recovered from the projected English Tense/VerbForm features. The full person×number×mood cross-product is fused into single endings and not fully separated. Recommendation: PROMOTE the tense/aspect/form cells; flag the fused person/number for a paradigm-table pass.
